headline: Tax Amnesty Programme Collects RM7.2B From Undeclared Income
context: LHDN's Special Voluntary Disclosure Programme 2.0 running from July 2025 to June 2026 collected RM7.2 billion in the first 6 months from 184,000 taxpayers declaring previously hidden income and assets.

cards.big: 184,000 taxpayers came forward with RM42 billion in undeclared income — paying just 17% average penalty rate
cards.sub: Honest taxpayers who paid full rates all along subsidised those who hid income and got amnesty discounts

cards.big: Malaysia's shadow economy estimated at 21% of GDP or RM382 billion — SVDP captured only 11% of estimated evasion
cards.sub: Tax gap remains at RM35-45 billion annually after amnesty — structural enforcement, not amnesties, closes gaps

cards.big: 82% of SVDP declarations came from T20 individuals and corporations — confirming wealthy tax non-compliance is systemic
cards.sub: Average declaration was RM228,000 in hidden income — these are not small traders but professionals and business owners

cards.big: LHDN's audit workforce of 2,800 covers 3.2 million active taxpayer files — ratio of 1 auditor per 1,143 files
cards.sub: Singapore's IRAS maintains 1:380 ratio — Malaysia's enforcement capacity is structurally inadequate

cards.big: Every tax amnesty sends the same message — hide your income, wait for amnesty, pay less than everyone else
Should Malaysia permanently end tax amnesties and invest the RM200 million programme cost in 1,500 new LHDN auditors instead?
